events.title: Mystery oil slick response on the Columbia River (Clifton Channel)
events.description: An oil slick report in the Clifton Channel area of the Columbia River triggered response actions including protective booming. Follow-up surveys reported limited shoreline impacts (light marsh staining and scattered tarballs) and cleanup activities around August 12.

location.name: Clifton Channel, Columbia River, Oregon, USA

dedupe.cluster_hint: NOAA Incident 7035 Columbia River Clifton Channel oil slick Aug 1994

events.title: Abandoned drums with possible waste oil found near storm drain in Baltimore
events.description: A report to USCG/MSO Baltimore described abandoned 55-gallon drums (some labeled as oils) and an old heating oil tank near a storm drain that fed a tributary of the Patapsco River. The situation was treated as an immediate environmental threat requiring removal/response actions.

location.name: Baltimore, Maryland, USA (Pulaski Highway service road area)

key_quotes.quote: thirteen 55-gallon drums ... near a storm drain

dedupe.cluster_hint: NOAA Incident 7040 Baltimore abandoned drums Aug 1994

events.title: Mystery shoreline contamination in the Detroit and Rouge rivers affecting US/Canada shores
events.description: Reports described shoreline impacts from an animal-fat-like substance along parts of the Detroit River and adjacent Canadian shoreline, with related observations in the Rouge River. Cross-border coordination was initiated under Great Lakes contingency arrangements while the substance and sources were investigated.

location.name: Detroit, Michigan, USA (Detroit River / Rouge River)

key_quotes.quote: shoreline impacts from an animal-fat-like substance

dedupe.cluster_hint: NOAA Incident 7037 Detroit River Rouge River mystery substance Aug 1994

events.title: Downed F-14 incident with JP-5 fuel risk in Big Porpoise Bay (Pamlico River)
events.description: A downed F-14 aircraft was reported burning in Big Porpoise Bay, creating risk of JP-5 fuel release and prompting response actions such as deploying sorbent boom and pads. The incident focused on preventing or limiting fuel contamination of surrounding waters.

location.name: Big Porpoise Bay, Pamlico River, North Carolina, USA

key_quotes.quote: downed F-14 ... JP-5 onboard ... sorbent boom/pads

dedupe.cluster_hint: NOAA Incident 7041 F-14 Big Porpoise Bay Pamlico River Aug 1994

events.title: Tropical Storm Hector rainbands affect western Mexico and Baja California
events.description: In early August 1994, Tropical Storm Hector tracked parallel to the Mexican coast in the eastern Pacific. Its outer rainbands moved across portions of western mainland Mexico and Baja California before the system dissipated.

location.name: Mexico (Mexico City capital proxy)

key_quotes.quote: Tropical Storm Hector tracked parallel to the Mexican coast... outer bands moving across portions of western mainland Mexico and Baja California.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1994-08-hector-mx-baja

events.title: Hurricane Ileana peaks south of Mexico before weakening over cooler waters
events.description: A tropical disturbance became Tropical Depression Ileana on August 10, 1994, strengthening into a tropical storm on August 11 and a hurricane on August 12. Ileana then weakened back to a depression by August 14 and dissipated later that day south of Mexico in the eastern Pacific.

location.name: Eastern Pacific south of Mexico (Mexico City capital proxy)

key_quotes.quote: ...organized into Tropical Depression Ileana on August 10, strengthened into a tropical storm on August 11 and a hurricane on August 12... weakened... by August 14 and dissipated later that day.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1994-08-ileana-epac

events.title: Tropical Storm Beryl makes landfall near Panama City, Florida with heavy rain
events.description: Tropical Storm Beryl formed in the Gulf of Mexico on August 14, 1994 and moved along the Florida Panhandle. It made landfall near Panama City late August 15, then weakened inland while tracking north and northeast, producing heavy rainfall and flood concerns along its path.

location.name: United States (Washington, D.C. capital proxy)

key_quotes.quote: ...Tropical Storm Beryl developed on August 14... making landfall near Panama City late August 15... producing heavy rainfall...

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1994-08-beryl-florida

events.title: Heavy rains cause flooding in southern Taiwan (Kaohsiung area)
events.description: Heavy rainfall caused flooding in southern Taiwan’s Kaohsiung area in mid-August 1994. The report described transportation and utility disruptions and said thousands of residents were stranded.

location.name: Kaohsiung, Taiwan

key_quotes.quote: Heavy rainfall caused flooding in southern Taiwan... disrupting transportation and utilities in the Kaohsiung area.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1994-08-kaohsiung-flood

events.title: Typhoon Fred causes severe flooding impacts in Zhejiang Province, China
events.description: Typhoon Fred struck eastern China around August 21–22, 1994, bringing strong winds, storm surge and torrential rain. Reports described widespread flooding and significant damage in Zhejiang Province, including around Wenzhou, with evacuations and fatalities reported.

location.name: Zhejiang Province, China (Hangzhou capital proxy)

key_quotes.quote: Typhoon Fred struck eastern China... causing widespread flooding and major infrastructure damage in Zhejiang Province...

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1994-08-typhoon-fred-zhejiang

events.title: Highway 41 Fire in San Luis Obispo County, California burns ~48,531 acres and destroys 37 homes
events.description: A wildfire in San Luis Obispo County, California (near the Tassajara Creek area and Cerro Alto Campground) burned about 48,531 acres and destroyed 37 homes. Contemporary reporting described residents returning as containment improved, and later coverage reported the fire was declared fully contained by Aug. 19, 1994.

location.name: San Luis Obispo County, California, USA

sources.headline: Residents Return to Area Charred by California Fire
California's Biggest Fire of 1994 Devastates Central Coast Area

dedupe.cluster_hint: Highway 41 Fire (San Luis Obispo County, CA) Aug 1994

events.title: Wildfire near Huntington Lake / Big Creek area prompts evacuations in Fresno County, California
events.description: A wildfire near Huntington Lake in the Big Creek area of Fresno County, California forced evacuations affecting residents, campers, and cabin communities. Reporting described heavy smoke and ash and repeated evacuation orders around Aug. 26–27, 1994, with later coverage indicating roughly 5,000 acres burned and no structures lost.

location.name: California, USA (Fresno County wildfire near Huntington Lake)

sources.headline: Fire Forces Evacuations Near Huntington Lake
Huntington Lake Fire Nearly Contained; About 5,000 Acres Burned

dedupe.cluster_hint: Huntington Lake / Big Creek wildfire (Fresno County) Aug 1994

events.title: Corral Fire / Blackwell Complex in west-central Idaho prompts evacuations from vacation communities
events.description: Wind-driven forest fires in west-central Idaho, reported as including the Corral (or Corral Complex) fire and the Blackwell Complex, prompted evacuations from areas including Burgdorf Hot Springs and Secesh Meadows near McCall. Reporting described erratic behavior and rapid growth, alongside closures affecting large areas of forest.

location.name: Idaho, USA (west-central fires near McCall)

sources.headline: Idaho Fires Drive Out Residents in Resort Areas
Fires Spread in Idaho; Resort Communities Evacuated

dedupe.cluster_hint: Corral/Blackwell Complex fires near McCall, Idaho Aug 1994

events.title: Little Wolf Fire in Montana threatens homes; Army and National Guard assist suppression
events.description: Reporting on western U.S. wildfires described the Little Wolf Fire in Montana at roughly 14,000 acres and threatening homes, with U.S. Army soldiers and National Guard troops assisting firefighting efforts. The coverage characterized challenging conditions with changing winds and humidity as crews worked on containment lines.

location.name: Montana, USA (Little Wolf Fire near Flathead National Forest)

dedupe.cluster_hint: Little Wolf Fire (MT) Aug 1994

events.title: Wind-driven fires in Northern California destroy structures and prompt evacuations near Kelsey and Rough and Ready
events.description: Reporting described multiple wind-driven fires in Northern California that destroyed more than 30 structures, closed roads, and prompted evacuations. The coverage highlighted a fire near Kelsey in El Dorado County and another near Rough and Ready in Nevada County where multiple buildings were lost.

location.name: California, USA (fires near Kelsey and Rough and Ready)

sources.headline: Wind-Driven Fires Destroy Buildings in Northern California

dedupe.cluster_hint: Northern California fires near Kelsey & Rough and Ready Aug 1994

events.title: USFWS proposes listing seven Apalachicolan-region freshwater mussels as endangered or threatened
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service published a proposed rule to list several freshwater mussel species from the Apalachicolan Region as endangered or threatened. The proposal cites restricted ranges and threats including impoundments and degraded water/benthic habitat quality associated with siltation, runoff, mining, pollutants, and waste discharges.

location.name: Eastern Gulf Slope drainages (Apalachicolan Region), Alabama/Florida/Georgia, USA

dedupe.cluster_hint: USFWS Federal Register proposed mussel listings (Apalachicolan Region)
dedupe.fingerprint: 1994-08-03_usfws_apalachicola_mussels_proposed_listing|Eastern Gulf Slope drainages (Apalachicolan Region), Alabama/Florida/Georgia, USA|1994-08-03

events.title: NOAA proposes critical habitat designation for Johnson's seagrass on Florida's Atlantic coast
events.description: NOAA/NMFS published a proposed rule to designate critical habitat for Johnson's seagrass (Halophila johnsonii) along Florida's Atlantic coast. The proposal identifies habitat areas and features considered essential for conservation and management.

location.name: East coast of Florida (Sebastian Inlet to central Biscayne Bay), USA

dedupe.cluster_hint: NOAA/NMFS Johnson's seagrass proposed critical habitat (Federal Register)

events.title: USFWS proposes 10(j) nonessential experimental population designation for Yellowstone gray wolf reintroduction
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service published a proposed rule to reintroduce gray wolves to the Yellowstone National Park region under ESA section 10(j) as a nonessential experimental population. The proposal describes the intent to reestablish a viable wolf population in the region under specified management conditions.

location.name: Yellowstone National Park region (Wyoming/Idaho/Montana), USA

dedupe.cluster_hint: USFWS Yellowstone gray wolf 10(j) proposed rule (Federal Register)

events.title: EPA final rule sets default sequence for mandatory Clean Air Act sanctions (40 CFR Part 52)
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency issued a final rule describing the order in which mandatory sanctions apply following certain findings under Clean Air Act section 179. The rule sets a default sequence in which offset sanctions apply first, followed by highway sanctions.

location.name: United States (federal)

sources.headline: EPA issues final rule setting the sequence of mandatory Clean Air Act sanctions (40 CFR Part 52)

key_quotes.quote: order in which mandatory sanctions apply after certain findings under Clean Air Act section 179

events.title: EPA direct final rule updates stratospheric ozone phaseout appendices and allocation corrections (40 CFR Part 82)
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a direct final rule correcting an omission in prior stratospheric ozone phaseout regulations. The rule also updates appendices listing Parties to the Montreal Protocol and Article 5 countries, with effectiveness contingent on adverse comments.

location.name: United States (federal)

events.title: FWS final rule exempts live farm-raised fish and eggs from most export requirements (50 CFR Part 14)
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service issued a final rule exempting exports of live farm-raised fish and fish eggs from several export requirements such as licensing, designated port use, and user fees. The rule retains marking requirements and notes enforcement focus on protected species and CITES-covered exports.

location.name: United States (federal)

sources.headline: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service final rule exempts live farm-raised fish and eggs from most export requirements except marking (50 CFR Part 14)

events.title: EPA direct final rule revises Acid Rain Program emissions monitoring certification deadlines (40 CFR Part 75)
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a direct final rule revising 40 CFR Part 75 to extend certain continuous emissions monitoring system (CEMS) certification deadlines for affected gas-fired and oil-fired units. The notice states the effective date is contingent on whether adverse comments are received.

location.name: United States (federal)

events.title: EPA final rule tightens RCRA controls on certain HTMR slag uses as anti-skid/deicing materials (40 CFR Parts 266, 268)
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency issued a final rule amending RCRA regulations affecting certain uses of high temperature metals recovery (HTMR) slag residues derived from listed hazardous wastes. The rule states these slag uses as anti-skid/deicing materials are not exempt from Subtitle C requirements unless applicable land disposal standards are met.

location.name: United States (federal)

sources.headline: EPA final rule amends RCRA requirements for hazardous waste-derived HTMR slags used as anti-skid/deicing materials (40 CFR Parts 266 and 268)
